feedback.text: I would like to thank the European Union for taking an interest in the sometimes abusive operation of the video games industry. Today, some consumers saw goods they bought becoming innacesable as a result of pressure from a non-European collective, Collective Shoot pressuring Visa, Mastercard, Stripe and Pay Pal. However, it seems unacceptable to me that games that I have paid for can be withdrawn from me and – worse – that private companies can decide what I am entitled to buy. It is up to European and national legislators to decide what is legal to consume, not to businesses. Moreover, it is a whole ecosystem of independent content creators that are severely penalised by Visa, Mastercard, PayPal and Stripe practices. However, they represent a significant part of a video – and more broadly digital – ecosystem that is a potential pillar for European economic development and soft power. More broadly, it is not clear that the European Union must act to regulate a sector that is vital for the lives of all its citizens, before businesses take over the right to decide what can or may not be bought in the territory of the Union.
